name: Northern Cabbage Kimchi
recipeIngredient: 4 Soaked forest mushrooms, cut into thin strips
4 Dried stone mushrooms, soaked, cleaned and cut into thin strips
2 Korean pears, cut into thin juliennes
3 small ginger roots, peeled and crushed
3 cups red pepper powder, made into a paste with water
1/2 cup pickled coriander, cut into narrow strips
1 1/2 lb beef brisket, boiled in 5 quarts water or beer
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the tougher outer leaves of the cabbage and save them for wrapping.
Cut each cabbage head into 2-4 sections lengthwise.
2. Soak cabbage sections and radishes in a brine solution made with 3 cups salt dissolved in 4 quarts water for 3-4 hours or until softened. Rinse with cold water.
3. Halve the radishes, reserving 2, and make slit cuts up to two-thirds of the way from the bottom.
4. Cut pears into thin juliennes.
5. Cut pickled fish, squid, and octopus into 1½-inch narrow strips.
6. Cut remaining radishes into thin julienne strips and mix with red pepper paste. Add all remaining vegetables, mushrooms, chestnuts, octopus, squid, and pickled fish; mix well. Toss with oysters and pear strips, then season with salt. This is the stuffing.
7. Pack the stuffing between layers of cabbage leaves, filling the slit cuts in radishes. Stack the stuffed cabbage and radishes in a crock. Cover with the saved outer cabbage leaves (from step 1).
8. After 2-3 days, add a mixture of salt, pickled coriander juice, pickled baby shrimp juice, and beef broth just enough to cover the contents.
